name: Metro Nashville Open Data
automation: Use public dataset APIs where available; filter by East Nashville ZIPs, council districts, corridors, and geocoded points.
publishUse: Confirmed data briefs, watchdog explainers, recurring charts, and source checks.
caveat: Some dashboards lag or update nightly. Treat counts as informational unless the page says otherwise.
nextStep: Build saved queries for 37206, 37216, East Precinct, District 5, District 6, and the Gallatin/Main/Woodland corridors.

name: Metro Council, Insite, agendas, and fiscal notes
cadence: Before each council meeting plus weekly
automation: Monitor agenda pages and legislation search for East Nashville districts, street names, rezoning, grants, and public hearings.
publishUse: Boring PDF, Loud Implications; plain-English council previews; voting aftermath.
caveat: Only publish pointed commentary after confirming the actual bill, amendment, meeting date, and sponsor.
nextStep: Make a council keyword list: East Bank, Gallatin, Main Street, Woodland, Shelby, Dickerson, McFerrin, Lockeland, Inglewood.

name: Metro Planning Development Tracker
automation: Scrape/export map items or manually capture changed parcels until a stable feed is wired.
publishUse: Development watch, restaurant/retail rumors, zoning explainers, parking fights before they bloom.
caveat: A map item is a lead, not a conclusion. Confirm with linked cases and public records.
nextStep: Create saved map areas around Gallatin, Main/Woodland, Five Points, East Bank, and Riverside Village.

name: Metro Codes ePermits and permit search
automation: Monitor permits by address, corridor, permit class, and business names from the restaurant watchlist.
publishUse: Openings, build-outs, closures, patios, signs, DADUs, weirdly expensive construction.
caveat: Permits can be preliminary, revised, abandoned, or boring in a legally meaningful way.
nextStep: Seed address watchlist for known restaurant/bar corridors and high-change parcels.

name: NDOT permits and street/lane/sidewalk closure map
automation: Monitor right-of-way permits, street closures, sidewalk closures, event permits, and parklets.
publishUse: Cone Watch, weekend traffic pain, sidewalk parent alerts, event road closure explainers.
caveat: Closures can shift fast. Include source date and update if the map changes.
nextStep: Turn the interactive closure map into a daily East Nashville short list.

name: MNPD dashboards, crime maps, active dispatch, and East Precinct
automation: Use public dashboards/datasets for patterns and links; avoid sensational single-incident posting unless there is strong public interest.
publishUse: Safety explainers, trend checks, crash hotspots, official follow-ups.
caveat: Avoid mug shots and private citizen names unless there is a strong, sourced public-interest reason.
nextStep: Create an East Precinct dashboard check that flags trends, not random scary blotter filler.

name: hubNashville 311 service requests
automation: Filter service requests by geography and category: potholes, trash, codes, sidewalks, streetlights, parking, animals.
publishUse: What the neighborhood is asking Metro to fix, ranked by irritation value.
caveat: Service requests are claims from residents until Metro verifies or closes them.
nextStep: Build recurring 311 Irritation Index with categories, blocks, and status changes.

name: East Nashville private Facebook group
cadence: Twice daily once access workflow exists
automation: Use supervised browser capture or approved exports. Store internal screenshots/notes; publish transformed summaries with clear chatter labels.
publishUse: What People Are Mad About, rumor radar, tip prompts, lost-and-found patterns, comment-section weather.
caveat: Do not treat private group chatter as confirmed fact. Attribute as community chatter and verify accusations before naming names.
nextStep: Create a capture template: post link, screenshot, author visibility, claim, contradiction, suggested label, publish/no-publish.

name: Reader tips inbox
automation: Convert every tip into a source item with confidence, risk, attachments, and follow-up action.
publishUse: Scoops, reader questions, correction prompts, community leads, sponsor leads.
caveat: Anonymous tips are leads. Accusations require records, named sources, or multiple credible confirmations.
nextStep: Create tips@ domain inbox and route submissions into the source desk.

name: Restaurant, bar, coffee, and venue Instagram watchlist
automation: Track public posts, reels, stories where available, bios, hiring posts, tagged locations, and comments from key accounts.
publishUse: Soft Launch Watch, menu sightings, closing rumors, event listings, patio judgments.
caveat: Stories vanish. Capture source notes immediately and link to permanent posts when possible.
nextStep: Build the first 75-account watchlist from restaurants, bars, coffee shops, venues, pop-ups, and food writers.

name: Venue and event calendars
automation: Monitor Do615, venue calendars, Metro Parks, library events, markets, and festivals.
publishUse: Weekend agenda, free/cheap list, family survival list, weird pick of the week.
caveat: Events change; link source and include posted date when possible.
nextStep: Seed Eastside Bowl, The 5 Spot, No Quarter, Lowbar, Basement East, Shelby Park, and library branches.

name: Nashville outlets and newsletters
automation: Search public web/RSS for East Nashville terms across Axios, Nashville Scene, WSMV, Eater, Tennessean, NBJ, City Cast, and local blogs.
publishUse: Link roundups, local angle explainers, follow-up questions, source triangulation.
caveat: Do not rewrite reported work as ours. Add local context, source credit, and a real East Nashville angle.
nextStep: Create search queries for East Nashville, Five Points, Gallatin Pike, East Bank, Shelby Park, Lockeland, Inglewood, and Riverside Village.

name: X/Twitter public search and officials
status: Needs API or monitored search workflow
automation: Monitor terms, local reporters, council members, Metro departments, food writers, venues, and high-signal residents.
publishUse: Breaking pointers, quote sourcing, link discovery, official statements.
caveat: Tweets are not facts by themselves unless from direct/official sources.
nextStep: Build a public list of official accounts and Nashville reporters; keep private citizen tracking lightweight.

name: Nextdoor East Nashville chatter
cadence: Manual weekly until compliant workflow exists
automation: Manual source notes only for now. Treat as chatter unless independently verified.
publishUse: Complaint patterns, service issues, rumor radar, resident question prompts.
caveat: High privacy sensitivity. Avoid naming private citizens from closed-platform posts.
nextStep: Wait until the Facebook workflow works, then decide whether Nextdoor is worth the risk and hassle.

name: Neighborhood associations and civic groups
automation: Monitor websites, newsletters, public Facebook pages, and meeting notes.
publishUse: Civic calendar, meeting previews, issue explainers, hyperlocal context.
caveat: Often useful but not complete; cross-check with Metro records.
nextStep: Add Lockeland Springs, Historic East Nashville, Inglewood, Cleveland Park, and Eastwood groups.

name: Local sponsor and classified submissions
automation: Turn sponsor inquiries, placements, and classifieds into editorially labeled commercial leads where appropriate.
publishUse: Clearly sponsored posts, local business announcements, classifieds, event promos.
caveat: Sponsored content must be labeled. Editorial leads from advertisers still need source labels.
nextStep: Add self-serve intake fields for source link, neighborhood, offer, date, and sponsor contact.
